beats.narration: In immersion testing, the water between probe and part is not just coupling. It is a setting you control deliberately.

payload.title: THROUGH THE WATER
payload.sub: The water column is a setting, not just coupling.

beats.narration: Sound is four times faster in steel than water. Set the water path to at least a quarter of the thickness. Echoes stay clear.
An acoustic lens focuses the beam to a tight spot: small flaws ring louder, grain noise drops. In steel, focus pulls roughly four times shallower.

payload.heading: What a focused transducer buys you

beats.narration: Curved surfaces act like lenses too. A convex bar spreads the beam. Aim through the center, and match your focus to the radius.

payload.heading: Curved surfaces bend the beam
payload.note: AIM THROUGH THE CENTER — MATCH FOCUS TO RADIUS

beats.narration: Water path a quarter of the thickness, focus shifts in metal, aim through the center. In immersion, the water is part of the probe.

payload.sub: In immersion, the water is part of the probe.
